1→--- 2→title: erk_shared Package 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: edited 5→read_when: 6→ - "deciding where to put new utilities" 7→ - "moving code between packages" 8→--- 9→ 10→# erk_shared Package 11→ 12→The `erk_shared` package (`packages/erk-shared/`) contains reusable code that can be shared between the main `erk` package and future packages. 13→ 14→## When to Use erk_shared 15→ 16→| Situation | Location | 17→| ----------------------------------- | ---------------------- | 18→| Code only used by erk CLI | `src/erk/` | 19→| Reusable utilities and abstractions | `packages/erk-shared/` | 20→ 21→## Package Structure 22→ 23→``` 24→packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/ 25→├── config/ # Configuration management 26→├── context/ # Context helpers for dependency injection 27→├── core/ # Core abstractions (PromptExecutor ABC) 28→├── gateway/ # Gateway abstractions (git, github, shell, etc.) 29→├── hooks/ # Hook utilities 30→├── learn/ # Learn workflow utilities
... [26 lines omitted] ...

368→- `ctx.plan_backend` — The `ManagedPrBackend` ABC interface. Use for all plan operations including `update_metadata()`, `post_event()`, `create_plan()`, `get_plan()`, `list_plans()` 369→ 370→After PR #8210, the `PlanStore` ABC was consolidated into `ManagedPrBackend` (formerly `PlanBackend`). There is no separate read-side interface. 371→ 372→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py, ManagedPrBackend --> 373→ 374→**Source:** See `ManagedPrBackend` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py`. 375→ 376→## TUI Exit-with-Command Pattern 377→

80→ 81→PR is marked ready for review. Standard review/merge flow. No body format changes in this stage. 82→ 83→## Key Functions 84→ 85→All in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr_lifecycle.py`: 86→ 87→| Function | Purpose | 88→| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 89→| `build_plan_stage_body(metadata_body, plan_content, *, summary)` | Build Stage 1 body: details-wrapped plan + metadata. Optional `summary: str \| None` prepends before `<details>`. Footer NOT included (needs PR number). | 90→| `build_original_plan_section(plan_content)` | Wrap plan content in `<details><summary>original-plan</summary>` section. Used by both Stage 1 and Stage 2. | 91→| `extract_plan_content(pr_body)` | Extract plan content from PR body at any lifecycle stage. Handles both details-wrapped and old flat format. Summary is NOT included in output. | 92→| `find_metadata_block(pr_body, "plan-header")` | Extract metadata block for preservation during stage transitions. | 93→ 94→## Separator Semantics

Refer to the source directly for current signatures and behavior. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/metadata-block-fallback.md:<!-- Source: erk_shared/plan_store/create_plan_draft_pr.py, create_plan_draft_pr --> /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/metadata-block-fallback.md:The `create_plan_draft_pr()` function in `erk_shared/plan_store/create_plan_draft_pr.py` orchestrates this: it creates a draft PR with a metadata-only body, then explicitly adds the first comment with the plan content. The comment is not auto-created by GitHub — erk creates it via `add_comment()` and records the `plan_comment_id` back into the issue body for direct lookup. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/metadata-field-workflow.md:**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/create_plan_draft_pr.py` /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/plan-backend-migration.md:<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/conversion.py, header_str, header_int, header_datetime --> /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/plan-backend-migration.md:See `header_str()`, `header_int()`, and `header_datetime()` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/conversion.py`. These typed accessors replace the old `extract_plan_header_*()` functions, taking `plan.header_fields` and a key constant as arguments. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/plan-backend-migration.md:**Canonical conversion points:** `github_issue_to_plan()` (issue-backed) and `pr_details_to_plan()` (PR-backed) in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/conversion.py` /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/plan-backend-migration.md:- [`ManagedPrBackend` ABC](../../../packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py) - Complete method reference

session.tool_result:
93→ 94→## Separator Semantics 95→ 96→Two distinct separators serve different purposes: 97→ 98→- **Content separator** (`

---

`, double newline each side): Between metadata block and content section. Found with `find()`. 99→- **Footer separator** (`
---
`, single newline each side): Standard PR footer delimiter. Found with `rsplit()`. 100→ 101→These are distinct: `find()` matches the first (content), `rsplit()` matches the last (footer). 102→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>
session.assistant: The `planned-pr-lifecycle.md` has two more `plan_store/` references in Source comments. The plan says to fix line 85 (done) but there are also inline Source comments at lines near 85 in the original file. Let me read those:
session.tool_result:
100→ 101→These are distinct: `find()` matches the first (content), `rsplit()` matches the last (footer). 102→ 103→## False Match Prevention 104→ 105→The content separator `

---

` can accidentally form when "Remotely executed" notes or other text end with a blank line followed by the footer delimiter `
---
`. This creates a false positive for `find()`. 106→ 107→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr_lifecycle.py --> 108→ 109→`find_metadata_block()` defends against this by validating that `<!-- erk:metadata-block:` appears in the prefix. If the marker is absent, the function returns None rather than treating the accidental separator as the real content boundary. 110→ 111→The asymmetric search strategy reinforces this: 112→ 113→- **Content separator**: `find()` — matches the _first_ occurrence (metadata is always at the top) 114→- **Footer separator**: `rsplit()` — matches the _last_ occurrence (footer is always at the bottom)
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

115→ 116→This means even if `

---

` appears mid-body, `find()` still finds the real separator first (which has the metadata marker above it), and `rsplit()` still finds the real footer last. 117→ 118→## Constants 119→ 120→**Source:** `PLAN_CONTENT_SEPARATOR`, `DETAILS_OPEN`, `DETAILS_CLOSE` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr_lifecycle.py` 121→ 122→## Self-Referential Close Prevention 123→ 124→Planned PR IS the plan. The `pr_id` from prepare_state is the PR's own number. Using `Closes #N` in the footer would be self-referential, causing the plan to close itself. All three consumers of `assemble_pr_body()` set `issue_number=None` when the backend is `github-draft-pr`.
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: PlanBackend Migration Guide 3→read_when: 4→ - "migrating exec scripts from direct GitHubIssues to PlanBackend" 5→ - "implementing LBYL pattern for plan existence checks" 6→ - "handling PlanNotFound vs PlanHeaderNotFoundError" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "calling update_metadata() on PlanBackend" 9→ warning: "Always check isinstance(result, PlanNotFound) before calling update_metadata()" 10→ - action: "catching PlanHeaderNotFoundError" 11→ warning: "PlanHeaderNotFoundError is an exception; PlanNotFound is a result type - use LBYL for the latter" 12→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:30 PT" 13→audit_result: clean 14→--- 15→ 16→# PlanBackend Migration Guide 17→ 18→When exec scripts need to read or update plans, use `PlanBackend` instead of direct `GitHubIssues` calls. PlanBackend provides typed abstractions with discriminated union error handling. 19→ 20→## Migration Pattern 21→ 22→**Before (direct GitHubIssues):** 23→ 24→```python 25→gh = require_issues(ctx) 26→issue = gh.get_issue(repo_root, issue_number) 27→# No type safety on failure case 28→``` 29→ 30→**After (PlanBackend):**
... [156 lines omitted] ...
52→ output_error("issue-not-found", f"Issue #{issue_number} not found") 59→## Error Type Distinction 61→Two different error types serve different purposes: 66→| `PlanHeaderNotFoundError` | Exception (RuntimeError) | Raised when plan exists but metadata block is missing | 68→**PlanNotFound** is checked with `isinstance()` (LBYL pattern). **PlanHeaderNotFoundError** is caught with try/except because it indicates a corrupted plan state that cannot be predicted. 78→except PlanHeaderNotFoundError: 80→ output_error("no-plan-header-block", str(e)) 81→except RuntimeError: 82→ # GitHub API failure 83→ output_error("github-api-failed", str(e)) 95→ result["issue_update_error"] = f"Issue #{issue_number} not found" 100→ except RuntimeError as e: 102→ result["issue_update_error"] = str(e) 153→`add_label()` raises `RuntimeError` if the provider fails or the plan is not found (unlike `get_plan`/`update_metadata` which use the `PlanNotFound` result type). 163→## Error Handling Asymmetry: get vs update 168→- **update**: caller typically wants to report failure and continue 178→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) -- the broader error handling pattern

1→--- 2→title: Plan Content Extraction Fallback 3→last_audited: "2026-02-17 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "extracting plan content from GitHub issue comments" 7→ - "debugging 'no plan content found' errors in replan or plan-implement" 8→ - "working with older erk-plans that lack metadata blocks" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "assuming plan content is in the issue body" 11→ warning: "Schema v2 stores plan content in the FIRST COMMENT, not the issue body. The body contains only the plan-header metadata block. See extract_plan_from_comment() for the extraction logic." 12→ - action: "checking only one location when extracting plan content" 13→ warning: "Always check both the first comment (plan-body metadata block) and the issue body before reporting 'no plan content found'. The replan command documents this explicitly in Step 4a." 14→ - action: "using extract_metadata_prefix() or extract_plan_header_block() for metadata extraction" 15→ warning: "These functions are deleted. Use find_metadata_block() from packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/core.py for extraction and render_metadata_block() for rendering." 16→ - action: "using PLAN_CONTENT_SEPARATOR for new code" 17→ warning: "Metadata blocks are now self-delimiting via HTML comment markers (<!-- erk:metadata-block:{key} -->). PLAN_CONTENT_SEPARATOR is retained for backward compatibility only — new code must not use it." 18→ - action: "assuming metadata is at the top of a PR body" 19→ warning: "PR body metadata position changed from top to bottom. Do not assume metadata is at the start of the body." 20→--- 21→ 22→# Plan Content Extraction Fallback 23→ 24→Plans use a two-location storage design with backward-compatible fallback. Understanding why content lives where it does prevents agents from looking in the wrong place and reporting false "no content" errors. 25→ 26→## Why Two Locations Exist 27→ 28→Schema v2 plans split content across two GitHub API objects for a deliberate reason: **fast querying vs. full content**. 29→ 30→| Location | What it stores | Why |
... [51 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Metadata Field Addition Workflow 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new field to plan-header metadata" 5→ - "extending plan metadata schema" 6→ - "coordinating metadata changes across files" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-05 20:38 PT" 8→audit_result: edited 9→--- 10→ 11→# Metadata Field Addition Workflow 12→ 13→Adding a new field to the plan-header metadata block requires coordinated changes across multiple files. This checklist ensures nothing is missed. 14→ 15→## 5-File Coordination Checklist 16→ 17→### 1. schemas.py -- Define the Field 18→ 19→**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/schemas.py` 20→ 21→Three changes required in this file: 22→ 23→- Add the field name as a new entry in the `PlanHeaderFieldName` Literal union type 24→- Add a module-level constant with a matching `Literal` type (e.g., `YOUR_NEW_FIELD: Literal["your_new_field"] = "your_new_field"`) 25→- Add validation logic inside `PlanHeaderSchema.validate()` -- follow the existing pattern of checking `if FIELD in data and data[FIELD] is not None:` then validating the type. Also add the constant to the `optional_fields` set in that method. 26→ 27→### 2. plan_header.py -- Thread the Parameter 28→ 29→**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/plan_header.py` 30→
... [53 lines omitted] ...
70→1. Run `make fast-ci` to catch type errors
The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/metadata-field-workflow.md has been updated successfully.

1→--- 2→title: ManagedPrBackend Migration Pattern 3→read_when: 4→ - "migrating exec scripts to use ManagedPrBackend" 5→ - "working with require_plan_backend" 6→ - "understanding post_event vs update_metadata" 7→ - "Phase 3 ManagedPrBackend consolidation" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "calling gh api directly in an exec script for plan metadata updates" 10→ warning: "Use `require_plan_backend(ctx)` + backend methods instead. Direct gh calls bypass the abstraction and testability layers." 11→ - action: "choosing between post_event and update_metadata" 12→ warning: "post_event = metadata update + optional comment. update_metadata = metadata only. Use post_event when the operation should be visible to users in the issue timeline." 13→--- 14→ 15→# ManagedPrBackend Migration Pattern 16→ 17→Pattern for migrating exec scripts from direct GitHub CLI calls to the `ManagedPrBackend` abstraction (formerly `PlanBackend`). Part of Objective #6864 "Consolidate Plan Operations Behind ManagedPrBackend". 18→ 19→## Context 20→ 21→Exec scripts historically used direct `gh` CLI calls to update plan metadata and post comments. The `ManagedPrBackend` abstraction (a Backend ABC, not a Gateway) provides a testable, provider-agnostic interface for these operations. 22→ 23→## Migration Pattern 24→ 25→### Before: Direct `gh` Calls 26→ 27→```python 28→# Old pattern: direct subprocess calls 29→subprocess.run(["gh", "api", f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}", ...]) 30→subprocess.run(["gh", "issue", "comment", str(issue_number), "--body", comment])
... [68 lines omitted] ...

/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/teleport-slot-awareness.md /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-slots/src/erk_slots/__pycache__/teleport_cmd.cpython-312.pyc /home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-slots/src/erk_slots/teleport_cmd.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/.git/logs/refs/remotes/origin/plnd/teleport-helpstring-docs-03-12-1330 /home/runner/work/erk/erk/.git/refs/remotes/origin/plnd/teleport-helpstring-docs-03-12-1330

1→"""Teleport a PR's remote state to the local branch. 2→ 3→Remote-first counterpart to 'checkout'. While checkout preserves local state 4→(reusing existing worktrees, keeping unpushed commits), teleport force-resets 5→the local branch to match the remote exactly. Use teleport when a remote agent 6→has pushed commits and you want your local copy to match. 7→ 8→Operates on the current worktree by default, with --new-slot to create a fresh 9→worktree slot. 10→""" 11→ 12→from contextlib import nullcontext 13→from dataclasses import dataclass 14→from pathlib import Path 15→ 16→import click 17→ 18→from erk.cli.activation import ( 19→ activation_config_activate_only, 20→ ensure_worktree_activate_script, 21→ print_activation_instructions, 22→) 23→from erk.cli.commands.checkout_helpers import ( 24→ ensure_branch_has_worktree, 25→ navigate_and_display_checkout, 26→) 27→from erk.cli.commands.pr.checkout_cmd import _fetch_and_update_branch 28→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 29→from erk.cli.help_formatter import CommandWithHiddenOptions, script_option 30→from erk.core.context import ErkContext
... [551 lines omitted] ...
